input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:
`.testsettings` files are **legacy** and no longer honored by modern MSTest. You need to migrate to a **`.runsettings`** file. Here's what your settings map to:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout` property | `<TestTimeout>` in `<MSTest>` |
| `AssemblyResolution` / deployment | `<DeploymentEnabled>` in `<MSTest>` |
| Code Coverage data collector | `<DataCollector>` in `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

Let me create the replacement:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / TestDirectory useLoadContext -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
    <!-- Replaces <Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" /> -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Assembly resolution / test deployment config
2. A 30-second test timeout
3. Code coverage data collector

They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file, which is the modern equivalent. `.testsettings` files are legacy and not supported by newer MSTest/vstest runners.

Let me create a `.runsettings` file that covers all three aspects.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / TestDirectory useLoadContext -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
    <!-- Replaces <Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" /> -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
Created `local.runsettings`. To wire it up, you have two options:

**Option A** — Add to your `.csproj`:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**Option B** — Pass at the command line:
```
dotnet test --settings local.runsettings
```

**Key migration notes:**
- **Timeout**: `<TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` gives a 30s per-test timeout, same as before.
- **Deployment**: `<DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>` matches the `useLoadContext="true"` behavior (tests run in-place without file copying).
- **Code Coverage**: Uses the modern `2.0` collector URI. The `<CodeCoverage>` configuration block lets you exclude test assemblies from coverage results.

You can safely delete `local.testsettings` once you've verified the new settings work with `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`.
